suite.name: Enhanced SEO Broken Links Testing
suite.description: Enhanced test cases for VCST-3156 covering identified gaps in broken links functionality

section.name: Multi-Store & Context Testing
section.description: Tests for multi-store isolation and cross-store validation

custom.preconds: Two stores configured: Store-A and Store-B

step.content: 1. Navigate to Store-A storefront and access non-existent URL: /store-a-broken-link 2. Switch to admin and select Store-A context 3. Verify broken link appears in Store-A broken links grid 4. Navigate to Store-B storefront and access non-existent URL: /store-b-broken-link 5. Switch to admin and select Store-B context 6. Verify broken link appears in Store-B broken links grid 7. Switch back to Store-A context 8. Verify only Store-A broken links visible
step.expected: Each store shows only its own broken links, ensuring proper isolation

custom.preconds: Two stores with different product catalogs

step.content: 1. Open broken link in Store-A 2. Attempt to set redirect to Store-B product URL 3. Click Save 4. Verify validation error prevents cross-store redirect 5. Set redirect to valid Store-A product 6. Verify save succeeds
step.expected: System prevents invalid cross-store redirects with appropriate validation

section.name: Language & Localization Testing
section.description: Tests for multi-language support and localization

custom.preconds:
Multi-language store with EN and FR languages
1. Access non-existent URL with EN language: /en/broken-product 2. Access non-existent URL with FR language: /fr/produit-cassé 3. Go to admin broken links grid 4. Verify both URLs appear with correct language indicators 5. Verify Language column displays "en" and "fr" respectively 6. Test language filter functionalityLanguage-specific broken links tracked separately with proper language indicators

Multi-language store with language-specific products
1. Create broken link with language "en" 2. Set redirect to English product URL 3. Save the redirect 4. Test redirect from English storefront 5. Verify redirect works to English product 6. Test same URL from French storefront context 7. Verify appropriate behavior for language mismatchLanguage-specific redirects function properly with correct language context

section.name: Bulk Operations & Performance
section.description: Tests for bulk operations and performance under load

custom.preconds:
Multiple Active broken links exist
1. Select 5 Active broken links using checkboxes 2. Use bulk action menu to change status to "Accepted" 3. Confirm bulk operation 4. Verify all selected items show "Accepted" status 5. Verify timestamp "Modified" updated for all items 6. Check that bulk operation count is displayed correctlyBulk status changes work correctly with proper timestamp updates

Environment capable of generating large datasets
1. Generate 1000+ broken links (via script or repeated manual creation) 2. Navigate to broken links grid 3. Measure and record grid load time (should be < 3 seconds) 4. Test sorting by Count column - measure performance 5. Test filtering by Status - measure performance 6. Verify pagination works correctly with large dataset 7. Test search functionality with large datasetSystem handles large datasets efficiently with acceptable performance metrics

section.name: Advanced Validation & Error Handling
section.description: Tests for comprehensive validation and error scenarios

custom.preconds:
Active broken link available for editing
1. Open broken link for editing 2. Enter redirect URL to non-existent product: /non-existent-product 3. Set status to "Resolved" 4. Attempt to save 5. Verify validation error appears indicating target doesn't exist 6. Enter redirect URL to valid, existing product 7. Attempt to save 8. Verify save succeeds with valid targetSystem validates redirect targets exist and prevents invalid redirects

Two broken links available for testing
1. Create redirect from /page-a to /page-b 2. Save and verify 3. Create redirect from /page-b to /page-a 4. Attempt to save second redirect 5. Verify system detects circular reference 6. Test redirect behavior from frontend 7. Verify no infinite loops occurCircular redirects are detected and prevented, system remains stable

Ability to simulate database connectivity issues
1. Simulate database connection failure (disconnect DB) 2. Attempt to load broken links grid 3. Verify graceful error message displays 4. Verify no application crash occurs 5. Restore database connection 6. Verify grid loads correctly after restoration 7. Test that no data corruption occurredGraceful error handling for database issues with user-friendly messages

section.name: Integration & End-to-End Testing
section.description: Tests for complete integration flows and GraphQL functionality

custom.preconds: Access to GraphQL endpoint and admin panel

step.content: 1. Execute seoSlug GraphQL query for non-existent URL via GraphQL client 2. Verify query returns empty result 3. Check admin broken links grid 4. Verify broken link event is logged with correct metadata 5. Execute same query again 6. Verify hit count increments in admin grid 7. Verify all GraphQL response fields are correct
step.expected: GraphQL integration correctly creates and updates broken link entries

step.content: 1. Access non-existent URL from frontend: /test-broken-flow 2. Verify 404 page displays to user 3. Go to admin and verify broken link logged 4. Set redirect to valid product page: /valid-product 5. Save redirect with "Resolved" status 6. Return to frontend and access original broken URL 7. Verify redirect works to target product 8. Verify hit count remains stable (no further increment) 9. Verify user experience is seamless
step.expected: Complete broken link resolution workflow functions end-to-end

section.name: Advanced Grid Operations
section.description: Tests for complex grid functionality and data management

custom.preconds: Broken links with various statuses, dates, and languages

step.content: 1. Apply status filter: "Active" 2. Verify only Active items show 3. Add date range filter: Last 7 days 4. Verify results match both criteria 5. Add language filter: "en" 6. Verify results match all three criteria 7. Clear filters one by one and verify results update 8. Test filter combinations with "Resolved" and "Accepted" statuses
step.expected: Multiple filters work together correctly and can be combined/removed

custom.preconds: Broken links data available for export

step.content: 1. Select subset of broken links (5-10 items) 2. Use export functionality to generate CSV/Excel 3. Verify export contains all required fields: Permalink, Status, Count, etc. 4. Verify export file format is correct and readable 5. If import functionality available, test importing the exported data 6. Verify data integrity after import process
step.expected: Export/import functionality works correctly preserving all data

section.name: Security & Permissions
section.description: Tests for role-based access control and security

custom.preconds: Different user roles configured: Admin, Editor, Viewer

step.content: 1. Login as Viewer role 2. Navigate to broken links section 3. Verify can view broken links but edit buttons disabled 4. Attempt direct access to edit functionality 5. Verify access denied appropriately 6. Login as Editor role 7. Verify can edit but not delete broken links 8. Login as Admin role 9. Verify full access to all functionality
step.expected: Proper role-based access control with appropriate restrictions per role

section.name: Backup & Restore Testing
section.description: Tests for data persistence and backup/restore functionality

step.content: 1. Create 5 broken links with different statuses (Active, Resolved, Accepted) 2. Set redirects for Resolved items 3. Record all metadata: counts, timestamps, etc. 4. Perform complete system backup 5. Clear/delete broken links data from system 6. Verify broken links grid is empty 7. Restore system from backup 8. Verify all broken links restored with correct data 9. Verify redirects work correctly after restore
step.expected: Backup/restore completely preserves broken links data and functionality
